mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS path lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation within the next month. When the policy rate is easing, the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day returns because lower real financing costs and higher risk appetite support multiples.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months where FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, inflation expectations rise faster than policy restraint, compressing real returns and equity multiples. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days until monetary tightening catches up.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
regime_hint: early inflation scare with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if inflation acceleration does not outperform CPI level changes alone in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE indicates weakening household income growth and higher recession risk, which reduces expected corporate earnings and raises equity risk premia. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in at least 55% of episodes after the 0.3 percentage point 3-month unemployment rise, or if the signal loses predictive sign in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS implies broad payroll income growth and stronger retail demand, which should lift revenue expectations for cyclical equities. The S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days when both series improve together.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase versus their 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-improvement condition does not yield positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if either PAYEMS or RSAFS alone explains the effect as well as the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and utilization, which supports cyclical earnings and cash-flow revisions. This should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon when industrial production is expanding steadily.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of two-month INDPRO expansion episodes, or if the signal reverses sign after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST weakens construction activity, employment income, and credit-sensitive demand, which foreshadows softer corporate revenues and lower equity prices. The effect should be most visible over the next month after a clear housing downturn begins.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of episodes after the 8% housing break, or if the effect vanishes when the housing decline persists for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and PAYEMS growth are both positive, the economy is expanding on both output and labor dimensions, which supports earnings breadth and multiple stability. This combined expansion should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth is positive and PAYEMS is above its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if positive joint-growth episodes fail to produce positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the combined signal is not stronger than either series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A low but rising unemployment rate following a period of very low joblessness indicates the expansion is maturing and profit margins face late-cycle pressure. In that regime, the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 60 trading days once labor slack begins to increase from a low base.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises from below 5.0% and PAYEMS growth slows versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the low-base unemployment rise does not precede negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal is positive during continued labor deterioration.
